introSection: Brontojet was a steel roller coaster built by German manufacturer Schwarzkopf which previously operated at Movieland Park in Lago di Garda, Veneto, Italy.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The coaster previously operated at Billing Aquadrome.

After it left Lightwater Valley, the plot was reused for Eagles Claw.

After being removed from Loudoun Castle after the 2004 season to make room for Rat, the coaster was put into storage. In 2005, it was listed for £85,000 but was not sold. It was instead refurbished and listed for sale again in October 2007. It was sold to Movieland Park in Italy where it opened on 31 July 2010.

sections.title: Loudoun Castle
sections.content: Yellow track and light green supports.

sections.title: Lightwater Valley
sections.content: Red track and dark green supports.

fullText:
Brontojet was a steel roller coaster built by German manufacturer Schwarzkopf which previously operated at Movieland Park in Lago di Garda, Veneto, Italy.

The coaster previously operated at Billing Aquadrome.

After it left Lightwater Valley, the plot was reused for Eagles Claw.

After being removed from Loudoun Castle after the 2004 season to make room for Rat, the coaster was put into storage. In 2005, it was listed for £85,000 but was not sold. It was instead refurbished and listed for sale again in October 2007. It was sold to Movieland Park in Italy where it opened on 31 July 2010.





Grey track and brown supports.

Yellow track and light green supports.

Red track and dark green supports.

Red track and white supports.
